# Decisions

## Daily review suggestion quality is directly proportional to component file completeness

The daily review pipeline reads the FULL TEXT of every component file (rules, skills, agents, hooks, memory, plugins) before sending the combined input to Opus for analysis. Sparse, empty, or poorly written component files reduce suggestion quality because Opus has less signal to work with. If daily review suggestions seem generic or irrelevant, the first diagnostic step is auditing component file completeness — not the review pipeline itself.

_Source: `scripts/op-daily-review.js`_

_Tags: `daily-review`, `opus`, `quality`, `component-files`_

## Sessions are never purged by the retention policy

The retention job NULLs tool_input/tool_response after 7 days (warm tier) and deletes events after 90 days (cold tier), but the sessions table is permanently retained. This is intentional: session-level aggregates (total cost, token counts, duration) must remain queryable indefinitely for dashboard summaries even after the underlying events are gone. Consequence: the sessions table grows unboundedly — monitor its size in long-running installations.

_Source: `CLAUDE.md`_

_Tags: `retention`, `sessions`, `sqlite`, `storage`_

## Post-extraction deduplication via case-insensitive title normalization (pending)

To resolve case-insensitive duplicates in knowledge_entries table: (1) normalize all titles to a single case convention (recommend title-case for consistency with UI), (2) update upsertKnowledgeEntry query to use LOWER() in lookup, OR (3) implement pre-merge dedup that canonicalizes titles before LLM extraction. Current state: detection query works (finds 56 duplicates), but no automated cleanup. Migration needed: merge entries with same LOWER(title), keep oldest entry ID, merge body/tags from newer entries, mark newer entries as 'merged'.

_Source: `src/db/knowledge-entries.js`_

_Tags: `knowledge-entries`, `deduplication`, `data-migration`_

## Knowledge Entries Event Batch Limit (maxEvents Default 50)

Knowledge extraction limits the event batch fed to the LLM to maxEvents (default 50) per prompt. This balances extraction quality (enough context) against token budget and latency. Configurable via knowledge_max_events_per_prompt in config.json. Prevents unbounded context growth for long prompt sequences.

_Source: `src/op-knowledge.js, config.json`_

_Tags: `knowledge-extraction`, `token-budget`, `configuration`_

## Post-extraction deduplication vs LLM-guided normalization

Two approaches to fix case-variation duplicates: (A) Add normalization to the LLM extraction prompt, instructing it to use consistent title casing ('sentence case: capitalize first letter only'), making dedup more reliable upstream. (B) Implement case-insensitive dedup in `upsertKnowledgeEntry()` by comparing `LOWER(title)` and merging any existing entry with the same normalized form. Recommend A + B for robustness: LLM instructions prevent the problem, dedup logic catches edge cases.

_Tags: `knowledge-extraction`, `llm-output-control`, `data-quality`_
